expectations.text: Report confirms no contradictions were found
expectations.evidence: Report opens with '3 files checked. No contradictions or inconsistencies found.' and the cross-reference table confirms all values align across documents.

expectations.text: Report does NOT fabricate issues that don't exist in the documents
expectations.evidence: Verified every claim in the report against the source files (VISION.md, SPEC.md, ROADMAP.md). All values (20–300 BPM, denominators 4/8/16, 20 presets, tap tempo, accent patterns) match the originals. The note about preset scope difference between VISION and SPEC is real — VISION says 'tempo + time signature combinations', SPEC adds name and accent pattern. Correctly labelled as enrichment, not contradiction.

expectations.text: Report lists the files that were checked
expectations.evidence: Report states 'Files: VISION.md, SPEC.md, ROADMAP.md' and the title references all three. These match the actual input files.

expectations.text: Report is concise — does not pad a clean result with unnecessary detail
expectations.evidence: Report is 27 lines including the table. Opens with a one-line verdict, provides a focused cross-reference table of key values, and closes with brief notes. No filler paragraphs, no restating obvious facts, no unnecessary caveats.

claims.claim: Tempo range is 20–300 BPM across all three documents
claims.evidence: VISION: '20 to 300 BPM', SPEC: '20–300 BPM', ROADMAP: '20–300 BPM'

claims.claim: Time signature denominators are 4, 8, 16 across all three documents
claims.evidence: VISION: 'Y is 4, 8, or 16', SPEC: 'Supported denominators: 4, 8, 16', ROADMAP: '4, 8, 16 denominators'

claims.claim: Maximum 20 presets is consistent across documents
claims.evidence: SPEC: 'Maximum 20 presets', ROADMAP: 'up to 20 presets'. VISION does not specify a number.

claims.claim: VISION.md preset scope is narrower than SPEC.md (enrichment, not contradiction)
claims.evidence: VISION: 'save and recall tempo + time signature combinations'. SPEC: saves name, BPM, time signature, and accent pattern. The spec adds fields the vision omitted — genuinely enrichment.

suggestions.assertion: Report is concise — does not pad a clean result with unnecessary detail
suggestions.reason: Subjective and hard to fail consistently. A report with a useful table (like this one) could be called 'padded' or 'well-structured' depending on the grader. Consider a concrete threshold like 'Report is under 40 lines' or 'No section exceeds 5 sentences'.

No assertion checks that the report correctly identifies the cross-document enrichment (VISION preset scope vs SPEC preset scope). This is the most interesting analytical finding and goes unverified by the current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the core outcomes well. The main gap is that nothing tests whether the report identifies genuine nuances (like the preset scope difference) — a report that simply said 'no issues' with no analysis would also pass all four assertions.
